body.query: What did Marie Curie win Nobel Prizes for?

json.query: What did Marie Curie win Nobel Prizes for?

message.content:
Marie Curie won Nobel Prizes in two different scientific fields, making her the first person to achieve this distinction. In 1903, she was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics, which she shared with her husband Pierre Curie and Henri Becquerel. This recognition was for their groundbreaking work on radioactivity, a term that Marie herself coined. Their research built upon Becquerel's discovery of spontaneous radiation and led to the identification of two new elements: polonium and radium.

Eight years later, in 1911, Marie Curie received her second Nobel Prize, this time in Chemistry. This award was given in recognition of her discovery of radium and polonium, as well as her subsequent isolation of radium and study of its remarkable properties. Her work in this field was particularly significant as it laid the foundation for the development of X-ray technology and cancer treatments.

Marie Curie's achievements were not only remarkable for their scientific importance but also for breaking gender barriers in a male-dominated field. She remains the only person to have won Nobel Prizes in two different sciences, cementing her legacy as one of the most influential scientists in history. Her discoveries revolutionized our understanding of atomic structure and radioactivity, opening up new avenues for research in physics and chemistry that continue to impact scientific advancements to this day.

In 1911, she received the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for her discovery of radium and polonium, as well as her isolation of radium and study of its properties. Marie Curie remains the only person to have won Nobel Prizes in two different sciences.", "entities": [{"name": "Marie Curie", "type": "Person"}, {"name": "Nobel Prize in Physics", "type": "Award"}, {"name": "Nobel Prize in Chemistry", "type": "Award"}, {"name": "Pierre Curie", "type": "Person"}, {"name": "Henri Becquerel", "type": "Person"}, {"name": "radioactivity", "type": "Concept"}, {"name": "polonium", "type": "ChemicalCompound"}, {"name": "radium", "type": "ChemicalCompound"}]}

json.query:
MATCH (n)
WHERE ((n.name IN $entity_names
      OR ANY(ename IN $entity_names WHERE toLower(n.name) CONTAINS toLower(ename))
      OR ANY(ename IN $entity_names WHERE toLower(ename) CONTAINS toLower(n.name)))
      OR toLower(n.name) IN [x IN $entity_names | toLower(x)]
      OR ANY(ename IN $entity_names WHERE size(ename) > 3 AND toLower(n.name) CONTAINS toLower(ename)))
  AND (n.tenant_id = $tenant_id OR n.tenant_id IS NULL)
WITH n,
     CASE
       WHEN n:Organization THEN 1.3
       WHEN n:Person THEN 1.2
       WHEN n:City THEN 1.1
       WHEN n:Museum THEN 1.2
       WHEN n:Technology THEN 1.0
       WHEN n:Disease THEN 1.0
       ELSE 0.9
     END as entity_priority
ORDER BY entity_priority DESC
LIMIT 10

OPTIONAL MATCH path = (n)-[r*1..3]-(m)
WHERE (m.tenant_id = $tenant_id OR m.tenant_id IS NULL)
WITH path, n as startNode, entity_priority,
     reduce(score = entity_priority, rel IN relationships(path) |
       score + CASE type(rel)
         WHEN 'A_CREE' THEN 1.5
         WHEN 'CAUSE_PAR' THEN 1.4
         WHEN 'CONNECTE' THEN 1.3
         WHEN 'PROTEGE_CONTRE' THEN 1.3
         WHEN 'ETUDIE' THEN 1.2
         WHEN 'UTILISE' THEN 1.2
         WHEN 'CIBLE' THEN 1.2
         WHEN 'EXPOSE_A' THEN 1.1
         WHEN 'VISE_A_LIMITER' THEN 1.1
         WHEN 'SOUS_ENSEMBLE_DE' THEN 1.0
         WHEN 'ETEND' THEN 1.0
         ELSE 0.8
       END
     ) as path_score,
     length(path) as path_length

WHERE path_score > (path_length * 0.3)

RETURN
  startNode.name as start_entity,
  [node IN nodes(path) | {name: node.name, type: labels(node)[0]}] as path_nodes,
  [rel IN relationships(path) | type(rel)] as path_relations,
  path_score,
  path_length
ORDER BY path_score DESC
LIMIT $max_results
json.hyde_document: Marie Curie won Nobel Prizes in two different scientific fields. In 1903, she was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics, shared with her husband Pierre Curie and Henri Becquerel, for their groundbreaking work on radioactivity. In 1911, she received the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for her discovery of radium and polonium, as well as her isolation of radium and study of its properties. Marie Curie remains the only person to have won Nobel Prizes in two different sciences.

parameters.query:
=SELECT summary, relevance_score, entity_names 
FROM community_summaries 
WHERE entity_names && ARRAY[{{ $('Neo4j Query Builder (Deep Traversal V2)').json.entities.map(e => "'" + e.replace(/'/g, "''") + "'").join(',') }}]::text[]
  AND tenant_id = '{{ $('OTEL Init').json.user_context.tenant_id }}'
ORDER BY relevance_score DESC 
LIMIT 5

json.hyde_document:
Marie Curie won Nobel Prizes in two different scientific fields, making her the first person to achieve this distinction. In 1903, she was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics, which she shared with her husband Pierre Curie and Henri Becquerel. This recognition was for their groundbreaking work on radioactivity, a term that Marie herself coined. Their research built upon Becquerel's discovery of spontaneous radiation and led to the identification of two new elements: polonium and radium.

Eight years later, in 1911, Marie Curie received her second Nobel Prize, this time in Chemistry. This award was given in recognition of her discovery of radium and polonium, as well as her subsequent isolation of radium and study of its remarkable properties. Her work in this field was particularly significant as it laid the foundation for the development of X-ray technology and cancer treatments.

Marie Curie's achievements were not only remarkable for their scientific importance but also for breaking gender barriers in a male-dominated field. She remains the only person to have won Nobel Prizes in two different sciences, cementing her legacy as one of the most influential scientists in history. Her discoveries revolutionized our understanding of atomic structure and radioactivity, opening up new avenues for research in physics and chemistry that continue to impact scientific advancements to this day.

In 1911, she received the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for her discovery of radium and polonium, as well as her isolation of radium and study of its properties. Marie Curie remains the only person to have won Nobel Prizes in two different sciences.", "entities": [{"name": "Marie Curie", "type": "Person"}, {"name": "Nobel Prize in Physics", "type": "Award"}, {"name": "Nobel Prize in Chemistry", "type": "Award"}, {"name": "Pierre Curie", "type": "Person"}, {"name": "Henri Becquerel", "type": "Person"}, {"name": "radioactivity", "type": "Concept"}, {"name": "polonium", "type": "ChemicalCompound"}, {"name": "radium", "type": "ChemicalCompound"}]}
